role_title: Senior Product Manager, Home Loans Consumer Experience

level_signals: Expected to present to executive leadership
Expected to own product strategy and roadmap independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or higher, ideally in a technical, finance, or quantitative field
Experience developing and launching features across both 1st and 3rd party platforms, while consistently prioritizing a high-quality user experience
Superior communication skills, written and verbal. Experience presenting to executive leadership
Experience delivering quality software that solves business and user needs
Strong analytical and data-driven mindset; able to dive into financial and operational metrics
Experience owning end-to-end product delivery, from discovery through launch and iteration
High bias for action, strong ownership, and comfort operating in ambiguous, fast-moving cross-functional environments
Passion for your users! You do right by them, always
Ability to work with remote teams

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience speaking to actual users, empathizing with them and gathering insights, and translating those insights into meaningful product enhancements

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska (due to insurance coverage issues)

scope.surface: End-to-end digital home loan consumer experience (digital funnel: discovery, application, completion)
scope.business_model: B2C digital lending / fintech national bank
core_responsibilities: Define and execute a product vision that brings differentiated innovation to market and helps set a new standard for digital home lending
Own and prioritize the product backlog for your engineering squad, writing detailed requirements and partnering closely with developers to deliver iteratively
Own instrumentation and data for your products to drive deep understanding, sharper product decisions, and new growth opportunities
Lead experimentation to accelerate learning, uncover member insights, and drive measurable customer and business impact
Identify and launch new functionality that reduces friction, improves application completion, and further differentiates SoFi's home loan offering from competitors
Collaborate cross-functionally to translate customer needs and market opportunities into product strategy and execution
Operate in a fast-paced agile environment, making clear trade-offs and decisions

green_flags: Clear product ownership described (end-to-end home loan consumer experience)
Cross-functional scope is broad and well-articulated
Encourages growth-minded, builder mentality — signals autonomy
Nice-to-haves list is short and reasonable, lowering the effective bar
Company is a national bank with large membership base — meaningful product impact
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed in the JD body despite mention of a listed range
Location/work arrangement is ambiguous — no city, office requirement, or explicit remote policy stated (only Hawaii and Alaska exclusions mentioned)
Only 3+ years minimum experience for a Senior PM title may indicate title inflation or unclear leveling
No mention of home loans or mortgage domain experience in requirements — unusual for a specialized lending role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title and responsibilities, not stated explicitly)
location.type (inferred as unclear — no office or remote policy stated beyond Hawaii/Alaska exclusion)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred true from mention of remote teams and no office requirement stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from language 'Final base pay offer will be determined based on individual factors such as the candidate's experience, skills, and location')
ambiguities: Location policy is entirely unstated — the JD mentions ability to work with remote teams and only excludes Hawaii and Alaska, but does not specify hybrid, onsite, or fully remote. Treated as 'unclear'.
Compensation range is referenced ('The base pay range for this role is listed below') but the actual numbers are absent from the provided JD content.
The role title is 'Senior' PM but minimum requirement is only 3+ years, which is low for a senior level — possible leveling ambiguity.
